metadata.title: Government Schemes [India & States] Current Affairs MCQs

questions.question: Capacity Building for Service Providers Scheme was launched by which ministry?
questions.explanation: Since its start in 2009–10, the Capacity Building for Service Providers Scheme has trained about 6,43,000 people, with over 84,000 getting jobs in the tourism industry. It is a Central Government scheme launched by the Ministry of Tourism to develop India’s tourism potential. The scheme focuses on skill development and certification for both organised and unorganised tourism and hospitality sectors. Training programmes are conducted across urban, rural, and tribal areas of the country.

questions.question: ASPIRE scheme was launched by which ministry?

options.label: B
options.text: Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises

options.label: D
options.text: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology

answer.label: B
answer.text: Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises

questions.explanation: More than 100 Livelihood Business Incubators (LBIs) have been approved across India under the ASPIRE scheme. ASPIRE stands for A Scheme for Promotion of Innovation, Rural Industries and Entrepreneurship and was launched in 2015 by the Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSME). The scheme aims to establish a network of LBIs to promote innovation and accelerate entrepreneurship. It focuses on skilling and re-skilling unemployed individuals, self-employed people, and wage earners in agro-rural sector technologies and other emerging areas.

questions.question: Which ministry is the nodal agency for implementing PM-SETU scheme?

options.label: C
options.text: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship

answer.label: C
answer.text: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship

questions.explanation: The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship (MSDE) has invited industry leaders to join the PM-SETU (Prime Minister Skilling and Employability Transformation through Upgraded ITIs) scheme. PM-SETU is a Centrally Sponsored Scheme approved in May 2025 with a ₹60,000 crore budget and launched on 4 October 2025. It aims to upgrade 1,000 government Industrial Training Institutes (ITIs) using a “hub-and-spoke” model, with 200 hub ITIs supporting four spoke ITIs each. Five National Skill Training Institutes (NSTIs) in Bhubaneshwar, Chennai, Hyderabad, Kanpur, and Ludhiana will be upgraded into global Centres of Excellence. The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship (MSDE) is the nodal ministry for the PM-SETU scheme.

questions.question: Foster Parent Scheme (Palak Mata-Pita Yojana), that was recently in news, was launched by which state government in 2009–10?
questions.explanation: The Foster Parent Scheme (Palak Mata-Pita Yojana) was launched by the Gujarat government in 2009–10 to support orphaned and abandoned children. The scheme provides monthly financial assistance of ₹3,000 to relatives or caregivers of children up to 18 years of age. Its aim is to ensure children grow up in a family environment instead of institutional care. Banaskantha district has emerged as a leading district in effective implementation of the scheme. A total of 2,818 beneficiaries in Banaskantha have received support under the scheme so far. Currently, 1,727 children receive ₹51.81 lakh per month as assistance. The support helps meet education, healthcare, and daily living needs of vulnerable children.

questions.question: What is the name of the scheme approved by DST to provide long-term financing to spur private investments in research and innovation?
questions.explanation: The Department of Science and Technology (DST) has approved Technology Development Board (TDB) and Biotechnology Industry Research Assistance Council (BIRAC) as the first Second-Level Fund Managers (SLFMs) under the Research Development and Innovation (RDI) Scheme. The RDI Scheme aims to provide long-term financing or refinancing at low or nil interest rates to encourage private investments in research and innovation. The scheme has a total fund allocation of ₹1 lakh crore.

questions.question: Which organization released the report on “Global Water Bankruptcy 2026”?

options.label: B
options.text: UN University Institute for Water, Environment and Health

answer.label: B
answer.text: UN University Institute for Water, Environment and Health

questions.explanation: The United Nations University Institute for Water, Environment and Health released a report on Global Water Bankruptcy. It warns that the planet has entered a Global Water Bankruptcy era. Water Bankruptcy means long-term water use has exceeded renewable supply and safe limits, causing irreversible damage. Rivers, lakes, aquifers, wetlands, soils, and glaciers are degraded beyond full recovery. This differs from water stress, which is high demand but largely reversible. It also differs from water crisis, which is temporary and manageable with restoration. Key causes include chronic overuse of water resources, dam-driven overexpansion, ecological degradation, and climate change intensifying existing water stress.

questions.question: Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana was launched under which campaign?
questions.explanation: Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana (SSY) was launched on 22 January 2015 under the Beti Bachao, Beti Padhao campaign of the Government of India. It is a small savings scheme where deposits earn government-notified interest, currently at 8.2 percent per annum. The scheme is low-risk, with the Government guaranteeing both principal and interest. SSY aims to support education and marriage expenses of the girl child. Since inception, over 4.53 crore accounts have been opened with deposits exceeding ₹3.33 lakh crore as of December 2025. The scheme promotes financial security, women empowerment, and social transformation.

questions.question: Which state government has launched Mukhya Mantri Eti Koli Duti Paat scheme?
questions.explanation: Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma launched the Mukhya Mantrir Eti Koli Duti Paat Scheme in Doomdooma, Tinsukia district. More than ₹300 crore will be given as one-time assistance of ₹5,000 each to more than 6 lakh tea garden workers. The scheme covers permanent and casual workers from 836 tea gardens across 27 districts. The government highlighted 200 years of Assam’s tea industry and the contribution of tea tribes.

questions.question: Pradhan Mantri Kisan SAMPADA Yojana (PMKSY) is implemented by which ministry?
questions.explanation: The Ministry of Food Processing Industries (MoFPI) implements the Pradhan Mantri Kisan SAMPADA Yojana (PMKSY), a Central Sector Umbrella scheme. As of 31 December 2025, 1,607 projects have been approved under PMKSY, with 1,196 completed/operational and 411 under implementation. Delays in implementation are mainly due to non-receipt of statutory clearances from authorities like Revenue, Town Planning, Electricity, Water, Pollution Control Board, and Licensing Authorities. Funds are released only after submission of required documents and fulfillment of all criteria under the scheme guidelines.

questions.question: What is the primary objective of the NALSA SAMVAD Scheme?

options.label: A
options.text: Ensure access to justice for marginalized tribal communities

options.label: C
options.text: Provide financial grants to rural entrepreneurs

options.label: D
options.text: Promote digital literacy in rural areas

answer.label: A
answer.text: Ensure access to justice for marginalized tribal communities

questions.explanation: National Legal Services Authority (NALSA) launched NALSA SAMVAD Scheme 2025 in April 2025 to ensure access to justice for Scheduled Tribes (STs), Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Groups (PVTGs) and De-Notified and Nomadic Tribes (DNTs). The scheme creates dedicated district-level SAMVAD Units and deploys trained lawyers and para-legal volunteers from tribal areas. It provides legal aid on land and forest rights, displacement, rehabilitation, documentation and welfare schemes. It promotes legal awareness through door-to-door outreach, camps and Gram Sabha coordination in local languages.
